openmeter/subject/service.purpose: Concrete implementation of subject.Service that orchestrates CRUD lifecycle for Subject entities via subject.Adapter, wrapping all mutating operations in transactions and fanning out lifecycle events through a models.ServiceHookRegistry. Reads bypass transactions; writes always use transaction.Run or transaction.RunWithNoValue so hooks and adapter writes are atomic.

patterns.name: Interface compliance guard
patterns.description: var _ subject.Service = (*Service)(nil) at package scope ensures compile-time satisfaction of the interface.

patterns.name: Input validation before adapter
patterns.description: Every mutating method calls input.Validate() and wraps errors with models.NewGenericValidationError before any adapter call.
patterns.example: if err := input.Validate(); err != nil { return subject.Subject{}, fmt.Errorf("invalid input: %w", models.NewGenericValidationError(err)) }

patterns.name: transaction.Run wraps writes and hooks
patterns.description: Create, Update, and Delete use transaction.Run / transaction.RunWithNoValue so adapter writes and hook fan-outs share the same DB transaction and roll back together on failure.
patterns.example: return transaction.Run(ctx, s.subjectAdapter, func(ctx context.Context) (subject.Subject, error) { sub, err := s.subjectAdapter.Create(ctx, input); s.hooks.PostCreate(ctx, &sub); return sub, nil })

patterns.name: ServiceHookRegistry fan-out in lifecycle order
patterns.description: Hooks fire in correct order: PostCreate after insert, PreUpdate before update + PostUpdate after, PreDelete before delete + PostDelete after. All hook calls propagate the transaction-carrying ctx.
patterns.example: s.hooks.PreUpdate(ctx, &sub); sub, err = s.subjectAdapter.Update(ctx, input); s.hooks.PostUpdate(ctx, &sub)

patterns.name: Read-only methods bypass transaction
patterns.description: GetById, GetByKey, GetByIdOrKey, and List delegate directly to s.subjectAdapter without transaction.Run to avoid unnecessary savepoint overhead.
patterns.example: func (s *Service) GetById(ctx context.Context, id models.NamespacedID) (subject.Subject, error) { return s.subjectAdapter.GetById(ctx, id) }

patterns.name: Constructor nil-guard on Adapter
patterns.description: New() returns an error if subjectAdapter is nil, preventing silent runtime panics from wiring mistakes.
patterns.example: if subjectAdapter == nil { return nil, fmt.Errorf("subject adapter is required") }

key_file_guides.role: Single-file service implementation holding all subject.Service method bodies, the ServiceHookRegistry field, and the RegisterHooks forwarder.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding adapter calls outside transaction.Run/RunWithNoValue in Create, Update, or Delete; omitting pre/post hook calls or firing them in the wrong order; using context.Background() instead of propagating the incoming ctx.

key_file_guides.role: Integration test for the full CRUD surface driven via subjecttestutils.NewTestEnv; exercises hooks indirectly through customer and entitlement interactions.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Must use t.Context() throughout — never context.Background(); env is built from concrete constructors (adapter + service), not app/common, to avoid import cycles.

anti_patterns: Calling adapter methods outside transaction.Run / transaction.RunWithNoValue in Create, Update, or Delete — breaks atomic rollback on hook failure.
Firing hooks outside the transaction boundary (after transaction.Run returns) — hook side-effects become non-atomic with the DB write.
Importing app/common in tests — use subjecttestutils.NewTestEnv which wires from concrete constructors to preserve test isolation.
Using context.Background() or context.TODO() instead of propagating the caller's ctx — drops Ent transaction driver and OTel spans.
Adding business logic directly in hook callback methods — delegate to a provisioner struct (see hooks/ sub-package) to maintain single responsibility.

decisions.decision: Transactions wrap both adapter writes and hook execution
decisions.rationale: Hooks may write to other tables (e.g. provisioning customer-subject rows); rolling back the transaction on hook failure reverts all writes atomically.

decisions.decision: ServiceHookRegistry is embedded as a value in Service, not an interface parameter
decisions.rationale: Hooks are registered at startup via RegisterHooks after Wire injects the service; embedding the registry avoids an extra constructor parameter and eliminates a nil-interface panic.
Reads have no side-effects and trigger no hooks; wrapping them in transaction.Run would add unnecessary savepoint overhead on every List/Get call.

code_examples.scenario: Implement a new mutating method on Service following the established pattern
